message:
chore(bench_cmd): migrate capability bench to fallible `try_constraints_cbor` + `verify_bound`

The capability builder/verifier surface in `fcp-capability` recently
moved away from infallible variants (`constraints_cbor`, `verify`) to
fallible/binding-aware variants (`try_constraints_cbor`, `verify_bound`)
that surface CBOR parse errors at construction time and bind the verify
call to a specific issuer/operation context. The `bench_cmd` capability
benchmark was still calling the legacy infallible methods, which
either fails to compile or silently calls a deprecated path.

* `bench_capability_verify` now builds the capability via
  `try_constraints_cbor(&constraints_cbor).expect("constraints should parse")`,
  matching how the rest of the codebase constructs benchmark fixtures
  whose inputs are known-good.
* The hot-loop call switches from `verify(...)` to `verify_bound(...)`,
  preserving the same arguments (`capability.clone(), &cap, &op, &[]`)
  but routing through the binding-aware path the production verifier
  uses.

No behavioral change to the benchmark itself; it now compiles against
the current capability API and exercises the same code path real
callers hit.
